sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
And, England, if my love thou hold’st at aught—
As my great power thereof may give thee sense,
Since yet thy cicatrice looks raw and red
After the Danish sword and thy free awe
Pays homage to us — thou mayst not coldly set
Our sovereign process, which imports at full,
By letters congruing to that effect,
The present death of Hamlet. Do it, England,
For like the hectic in my blood he rages,
And thou must cure me. Till I know ’tis done,
Howe’er my haps, my joys were ne’er begun.

FORTINBRAS enters with his army and a CAPTAIN.

sentences.speaker: FORTINBRAS
sentences.text:
Go, Captain, from me greet the Danish king
Tell him that, by his license, Fortinbras
Craves the conveyance of a promised march
Over his kingdom. You know the rendezvous.
If that his majesty would aught with us,
We shall express our duty in his eye,
And let him know so.

sentences.speaker: FORTINBRAS
sentences.text:

And let him know so.

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text: I will do ’t, my lord.

sentences.speaker: FORTINBRAS
sentences.text: Go softly on.
All except the CAPTAIN exits.
HAMLET, ROSENCRANTZ, GUILDENSTERN, and others enter.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: Good sir, whose powers are these?

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text: They are of Norway, sir.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: How purposed, sir, I pray you?

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text: Against some part of Poland.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: Who commands them, sir?

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text: The nephew to old Norway, Fortinbras.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Goes it against the main of Poland, sir,
Or for some frontier?

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text:
Truly to speak, and with no addition,
We go to gain a little patch of ground
That hath in it no profit but the name.
To pay five ducats, five, I would not farm it.
Nor will it yield to Norway or the Pole
A ranker rate, should it be sold in fee.

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text:

To pay five ducats, five, I would not farm it.
Nor will it yield to Norway or the Pole
A ranker rate, should it be sold in fee.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: Why, then the Polack never will defend it.

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text: Yes, it is already garrisoned.

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text: Yes, it is already garrisoned.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Two thousand souls and twenty thousand ducats
Will not debate the question of this straw.
This is th’ impostume of much wealth and peace,
That inward breaks and shows no cause without
Why the man dies. —I humbly thank you, sir.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:

Why the man dies. —I humbly thank you, sir.

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text: God be wi’ you, sir.
The CAPTAIN exits.

sentences.speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
sentences.text: Will ’t please you go, my lord?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: I’ll be with you straight. Go a little before.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: I’ll be with you straight. Go a little before.
Everyone exits except HAMLET.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
How all occasions do inform against me,
And spur my dull revenge! What is a man
If his chief good and market of his time
Be but to sleep and feed? A beast, no more.
Sure, he that made us with such large discourse,
Looking before and after, gave us not
That capability and godlike reason
To fust in us unused. Now, whether it be
Bestial oblivion, or some craven scruple
Of thinking too precisely on th’ event—
A thought which, quartered, hath but one part wisdom
And ever three parts coward—I do not know
Why yet I live to say “This thing’s to do,”
Sith I have cause and will and strength and means
To do ’t. Examples gross as earth exhort me.
Witness this army of such mass and charge
Led by a delicate and tender prince,
Whose spirit with divine ambition puffed
Makes mouths at the invisible event,
Exposing what is mortal and unsure
To all that fortune, death, and danger dare,
Even for an eggshell. Rightly to be great
Is not to stir without great argument,
But greatly to find quarrel in a straw
When honor’s at the stake. How stand I then,
That have a father killed, a mother stained,
Excitements of my reason and my blood,
And let all sleep —while, to my shame, I see
The imminent death of twenty thousand men,
That for a fantasy and trick of fame
Go to their graves like beds, fight for a plot
Whereon the numbers cannot try the cause,
Which is not tomb enough and continent
To hide the slain? Oh, from this time forth,
My thoughts be bloody, or be nothing worth!

To fust in us unused. Now, whether it be
Bestial oblivion, or some craven scruple
Of thinking too precisely on th’ event—
A thought which, quartered, hath but one part wisdom
And ever three parts coward—I do not know
Why yet I live to say “This thing’s to do,”
Sith I have cause and will and strength and means
To do ’t. Examples gross as earth exhort me.
Witness this army of such mass and charge
Led by a delicate and tender prince,
Whose spirit with divine ambition puffed
Makes mouths at the invisible event,
Exposing what is mortal and unsure
To all that fortune, death, and danger dare,
Even for an eggshell. Rightly to be great
Is not to stir without great argument,
But greatly to find quarrel in a straw
When honor’s at the stake. How stand I then,
That have a father killed, a mother stained,
Excitements of my reason and my blood,
And let all sleep —while, to my shame, I see
The imminent death of twenty thousand men,
That for a fantasy and trick of fame
Go to their graves like beds, fight for a plot
Whereon the numbers cannot try the cause,
Which is not tomb enough and continent
To hide the slain? Oh, from this time forth,
My thoughts be bloody, or be nothing worth!
He exits.
HORATIO, GERTRUDE, and a GENTLEMAN enter.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: I will not speak with her.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
She is importunate,
Indeed distract. Her mood will needs be pitied.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
She is importunate,
Indeed distract. Her mood will needs be pitied.

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
She is importunate,
Indeed distract. Her mood will needs be pitied.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: What would she have?

sentences.speaker: GENTLEMAN
sentences.text:
She speaks much of her father, says she hears
There’s tricks i’ th’ world, and hems, and beats her
heart,
Spurns enviously at straws, speaks things in doubt
That carry but half sense. Her speech is nothing,
Yet the unshaped use of it doth move
The hearers to collection. They aim at it,
And botch the words up fit to their own thoughts,
Which, as her winks and nods and gestures yield them,
Indeed would make one think there might be thought,
Though nothing sure, yet much unhappily.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:
‘Twere good she were spoken with, for she may strew
Dangerous conjectures in ill-breeding minds.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: Let her come in.
The GENTLEMAN exits.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text:
[aside]
To my sick soul (as sin’s true nature is)
Each toy seems prologue to some great amiss.
So full of artless jealousy is guilt,
It spills itself in fearing to be spilt.

OPHELIA enters, insane.

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text: Where is the beauteous majesty of Denmark?

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: How now, Ophelia?

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
[sings]
How should I your true love know
From another one?
By his cockle hat and staff,
And his sandal shoon.

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
[sings]
How should I your true love know
From another one?
By his cockle hat and staff,
And his sandal shoon.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: Alas, sweet lady, what imports this song?

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
Say you? Nay, pray you, mark.
[sings]
He is dead and gone, lady,
He is dead and gone,
At his head a grass-green turf,
At his heels a stone.
Oh, ho!

Oh, ho!

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: Nay, but, Ophelia—

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
Pray you, mark.
[sings]
White his shroud as the mountain snow—
CLAUDIUS enters.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: Alas, look here, my lord.

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
[sings]
Larded all with sweet flowers,
Which bewept to the ground did not go
With true-love showers.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: How do you, pretty lady?

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
Well, God’ield you! They say the owl was a baker’s
daughter. Lord, we know what we are, but know not what
we may be. God be at your table.

we may be. God be at your table.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: Conceit upon her father.

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
Pray you, let’s have no words of this, but when they
ask you what it means, say you this:
[sings]
Tomorrow is Saint Valentine’s day,
All in the morning betime,
And I a maid at your window,
To be your Valentine.
Then up he rose, and donned his clothes,
And dupped the chamber door.
Let in the maid that out a maid
Never departed more.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: Pretty Ophelia—

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
Indeed, without an oath I’ll make an end on ’t:
[sings]
By Gis and by Saint Charity,
Alack, and fie, for shame!
Young men will do ’t, if they come to ’t.
By Cock, they are to blame.
Quoth she, “Before you tumbled me,
You promised me to wed.”
He answers,
“So would I ha’ done, by yonder sun,
An thou hadst not come to my bed.”

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: How long hath she been thus?

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
I hope all will be well. We must be patient, but I
cannot choose but weep, to think they should lay him i’
th’ cold ground. My brother shall know of it, and so I
thank you for your good counsel. Come, my coach! Good
night, ladies. Good night, sweet ladies. Good night,
good night.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: Follow her close. Give her good watch, I pray you.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: Follow her close. Give her good watch, I pray you.
HORATIO exits.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
Oh, this is the poison of deep grief. It springs
All from her father’s death, and now behold!
O Gertrude, Gertrude,
When sorrows come, they come not single spies
But in battalions. First, her father slain.
Next, your son gone, and he most violent author
Of his own just remove. The people muddied,
Thick, and unwholesome in their thoughts and whispers
For good Polonius’ death, and we have done but greenly
In hugger-mugger to inter him. Poor Ophelia
Divided from herself and her fair judgment,
Without the which we are pictures, or mere beasts.
Last—and as much containing as all these—
Her brother is in secret come from France,
Feeds on his wonder, keeps himself in clouds,
And wants not buzzers to infect his ear
With pestilent speeches of his father’s death,
Wherein necessity, of matter beggared,
Will nothing stick our person to arraign
In ear and ear. O my dear Gertrude, this,
Like to a murdering piece, in many places
Gives me superfluous death.

A noise offstage.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: Alack, what noise is this?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: Where are my Switzers? Let them guard the door.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: Where are my Switzers? Let them guard the door.
A MESSENGER enters.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: What is the matter?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Save yourself, my lord.
The ocean, overpeering of his list,
Eats not the flats with more impiteous haste
Than young Laertes, in a riotous head,
O’erbears your officers. The rabble call him “lord”
And—as the world were now but to begin,
Antiquity forgot, custom not known,
The ratifiers and props of every word—
They cry, “Choose we! Laertes shall be king!”
Caps, hands, and tongues applaud it to the clouds:
“Laertes shall be king, Laertes king!”

They cry, “Choose we! Laertes shall be king!”
Caps, hands, and tongues applaud it to the clouds:
“Laertes shall be king, Laertes king!”

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Save yourself, my lord.
The ocean, overpeering of his list,
Eats not the flats with more impiteous haste
Than young Laertes, in a riotous head,
O’erbears your officers. The rabble call him “lord”
And—as the world were now but to begin,
Antiquity forgot, custom not known,
The ratifiers and props of every word—
They cry, “Choose we! Laertes shall be king!”
Caps, hands, and tongues applaud it to the clouds:
“Laertes shall be king, Laertes king!”

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text:
How cheerfully on the false trail they cry.
O, this is counter, you false Danish dogs!

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text:
How cheerfully on the false trail they cry.
O, this is counter, you false Danish dogs!
A noise offstage.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: The doors are broke.
LAERTES enters with others.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: Where is this king?—Sirs, stand you all without.

sentences.speaker: ALL
sentences.text: No, let’s come in!

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: I pray you, give me leave.

sentences.speaker: ALL
sentences.text: We will, we will.
LAERTES’ FOLLOWERS exit.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
I thank you. Keep the door.—O thou vile king,
Give me my father!

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
I thank you. Keep the door.—O thou vile king,
Give me my father!

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: Calmly, good Laertes.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
That drop of blood that’s calm proclaims me bastard,
Cries “Cuckold!” to my father, brands the “harlot”
Even here between the chaste unsmirchèd brow
Of my true mother.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
What is the cause, Laertes,
That thy rebellion looks so giant-like?—
Let him go, Gertrude. Do not fear our person.
There’s such divinity doth hedge a king
That treason can but peep to what it would,
Acts little of his will. —Tell me, Laertes,
Why thou art thus incensed. —Let him go, Gertrude.—
Speak, man.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS

Speak, man.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: Where is my father?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: Dead.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: But not by him.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: Let him demand his fill.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
How came he dead? I’ll not be juggled with.
To hell, allegiance! Vows, to the blackest devil!
Conscience and grace, to the profoundest pit!
I dare damnation. To this point I stand
That both the worlds I give to negligence.
Let come what comes, only I’ll be revenged
Most thoroughly for my father.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: Who shall stay you?

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
My will, not all the world.
And for my means, I’ll husband them so well,
They shall go far with little.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
My will, not all the world.
And for my means, I’ll husband them so well,
They shall go far with little.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
Good Laertes,
If you desire to know the certainty
Of your dear father’s death, is ’t writ in your
revenge,
That, swoopstake, you will draw both friend and foe,
Winner and loser?

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: None but his enemies.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: Will you know them then?

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
To his good friends thus wide I’ll ope my arms
And, like the kind life-rendering pelican,
Repast them with my blood.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
Why, now you speak
Like a good child and a true gentleman.
That I am guiltless of your father’s death
And am most sensible in grief for it,
It shall as level to your judgment pierce
As day does to your eye.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: How now? What noise is that?

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: How now? What noise is that?
OPHELIA enters.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
O heat, dry up my brains! Tears seven times salt,
Burn out the sense and virtue of mine eye!
By heaven, thy madness shall be paid by weight,
Till our scale turn the beam. O rose of May,
Dear maid, kind sister, sweet Ophelia!
O heavens, is ’t possible a young maid’s wits
Should be as mortal as an old man’s life?
Nature is fine in love, and where ’tis fine,
It sends some precious instance of itself
After the thing it loves.

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
[sings]
They bore him barefaced on the bier,
Hey, non nonny, nonny, hey, nonny,
And in his grave rained many a tear.
Fare you well, my dove.

[sings]

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
Hadst thou thy wits, and didst persuade revenge,
It could not move thus.

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
You must sing A-down a-down—And you, Call him a-
down-a—Oh, how the wheel becomes it! It is the false
steward that stole his master’s daughter.

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
You must sing A-down a-down—And you, Call him a-
down-a—Oh, how the wheel becomes it! It is the false
steward that stole his master’s daughter.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: This nothing’s more than matter.

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
There’s rosemary, that’s for remembrance. Pray you,
love, remember. And there is pansies, that’s for
thoughts.

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
There’s rosemary, that’s for remembrance. Pray you,
love, remember. And there is pansies, that’s for
thoughts.

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
There’s rosemary, that’s for remembrance. Pray you,
love, remember. And there is pansies, that’s for
thoughts.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: A document in madness. Thoughts and remembrance fitted.

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
There’s fennel for you, and columbines.—There’s rue for
you, and here’s some for me. We may call it “herb of
grace” o’ Sundays.—Oh, you must wear your rue with a
difference.— There’s a daisy. I would give you some
violets, but they withered all when my father died. They
say he made a good end [sings] For bonny sweet Robin is
all my joy—

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
Thought and affliction, passion, hell itself,
She turns to favor and to prettiness.

sentences.speaker: OPHELIA
sentences.text:
[sings]
And will he not come again?
And will he not come again?
No, no, he is dead,
Go to thy deathbed.
He never will come again.
His beard was as white as snow,
All flaxen was his poll.
He is gone, he is gone,
And we cast away moan,
God ha’ mercy on his soul. —
And of all Christian souls, I pray God. God be wi’ ye.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: Do you see this, O God?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
Laertes, I must commune with your grief,
Or you deny me right. Go but apart,
Make choice of whom your wisest friends you will.
And they shall hear and judge ’twixt you and me.
If by direct or by collateral hand
They find us touched, we will our kingdom give,
Our crown, our life, and all that we call ours,
To you in satisfaction. But if not,
Be you content to lend your patience to us,
And we shall jointly labor with your soul
To give it due content.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
Let this be so.
His means of death, his obscure funeral—
No trophy, sword, nor hatchment o’er his bones,
No noble rite nor formal ostentation—
Cry to be heard as ’twere from heaven to earth,
That I must call ’t in question.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:

Cry to be heard as ’twere from heaven to earth,

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
So you shall.
And where the offense is, let the great ax fall.
I pray you, go with me.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
So you shall.
And where the offense is, let the great ax fall.
I pray you, go with me.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
So you shall.
And where the offense is, let the great ax fall.
I pray you, go with me.
They exit.
HORATIO and a SERVANT enter.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: What are they that would speak with me?

sentences.speaker: SERVANT
sentences.text: Seafaring men, sir. They say they have letters for you.

sentences.speaker: SERVANT
sentences.text: Seafaring men, sir. They say they have letters for you.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: Let them come in.
SERVANT exits.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:
I do not know from what part of the world
I should be greeted, if not from Lord Hamlet.
SAILORS enter.

sentences.speaker: SAILOR
sentences.text: God bless you, sir.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: Let him bless thee too.

sentences.speaker: SAILOR
sentences.text:
He shall, sir, an ’t please him. There’s a letter for
you, sir— it comes from the ambassador that was bound
for England—if your name be Horatio, as I am let to know
it is. [He gives HORATIO a letter]

sentences.speaker: SAILOR
sentences.text:
He shall, sir, an ’t please him. There’s a letter for
you, sir— it comes from the ambassador that was bound
for England—if your name be Horatio, as I am let to know
it is. [He gives HORATIO a letter]

sentences.speaker: SAILOR
sentences.text:
He shall, sir, an ’t please him. There’s a letter for
you, sir— it comes from the ambassador that was bound
for England—if your name be Horatio, as I am let to know
it is. [He gives HORATIO a letter]

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:
[reads]
“Horatio,
When thou shalt have overlooked this, give these
fellows some means to the king. They have letters for
him. Ere we were two days old at sea, a pirate of very
warlike appointment gave us chase. Finding ourselves too
slow of sail, we put on a compelled valor, and in the
grapple I boarded them. On the instant, they got clear
of our ship, so I alone became their prisoner. They have
dealt with me like thieves of mercy, but they knew what
they did; I am to do a good turn for them. Let the king
have the letters I have sent, and repair thou to me
with as much speed as thou wouldst fly death. I have
words to speak in thine ear will make thee dumb, yet are
they much too light for the bore of the matter. These

They exit.
CLAUDIUS and LAERTES enter.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
Now must your conscience my acquaintance seal,
And you must put me in your heart for friend,
Sith you have heard, and with a knowing ear,
That he which hath your noble father slain
Pursued my life.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
It well appears. But tell me
Why you proceeded not against these feats,
So criminal and so capital in nature,
As by your safety, wisdom, all things else,
You mainly were stirred up.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
It well appears. But tell me
Why you proceeded not against these feats,
So criminal and so capital in nature,
As by your safety, wisdom, all things else,
You mainly were stirred up.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
Oh, for two special reasons,
Which may to you perhaps seem much unsinewed,
But yet to me they are strong. The queen his mother
Lives almost by his looks, and for myself—
My virtue or my plague, be it either which—
She’s so conjunctive to my life and soul,
That, as the star moves not but in his sphere,
I could not but by her. The other motive
Why to a public count I might not go,
Is the great love the general gender bear him,
Who, dipping all his faults in their affection,
Would, like the spring that turneth wood to stone,
Convert his gyves to graces —so that my arrows,
Too slightly timbered for so loud a wind,
Would have reverted to my bow again,
And not where I had aimed them.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
And so have I a noble father lost,
A sister driven into desperate terms,
Whose worth, if praises may go back again,
Stood challenger on mount of all the age
For her perfections. But my revenge will come.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
Break not your sleeps for that. You must not think
That we are made of stuff so flat and dull
That we can let our beard be shook with danger
And think it pastime. You shortly shall hear more.
I loved your father, and we love ourself.
And that, I hope, will teach you to imagine—

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: How now, what news?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: How now, what news?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Letters, my lord, from Hamlet.
This to your majesty, this to the queen. [gives
CLAUDIUS letters]

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Letters, my lord, from Hamlet.
This to your majesty, this to the queen. [gives
CLAUDIUS letters]

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: From Hamlet? Who brought them?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: From Hamlet? Who brought them?

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER
sentences.text:
Sailors, my lord, they say. I saw them not.
They were given me by Claudio. He received them
Of him that brought them.

sentences.speaker: MESSENGER

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: Laertes, you shall hear them.—Leave us.
The MESSENGER exits.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
[reads]
“High and mighty,
You shall know I am set naked on your kingdom. Tomorrow
shall I beg leave to see your kingly eyes, when I
shall, first asking your pardon thereunto, recount the
occasion of my sudden and more strange return.
Hamlet.”
What should this mean? Are all the rest come back? Or
is it some abuse, and no such thing?

is it some abuse, and no such thing?

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: Know you the hand?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
‘Tis Hamlet’s character. “Naked”?
And in a postscript here, he says “alone.”
Can you advise me?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
‘Tis Hamlet’s character. “Naked”?
And in a postscript here, he says “alone.”
Can you advise me?

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
I’m lost in it, my lord. But let him come.
It warms the very sickness in my heart
That I shall live and tell him to his teeth,
“Thus diddest thou.”

sentences.text:
If it be so, Laertes—
As how should it be so? How otherwise?—
Will you be ruled by me?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
If it be so, Laertes—

Will you be ruled by me?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
If it be so, Laertes—

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
Ay, my lord—
So you will not o’errule me to a peace.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
To thine own peace. If he be now returned,
As checking at his voyage, and that he means
No more to undertake it, I will work him
To an exploit, now ripe in my devise,
Under the which he shall not choose but fall.
And for his death no wind of blame shall breathe,
But even his mother shall uncharge the practice
And call it accident.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
My lord, I will be ruled
The rather if you could devise it so
That I might be the organ.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:

The rather if you could devise it so
That I might be the organ.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
It falls right.
You have been talked of since your travel much—
And that in Hamlet’s hearing—for a quality
Wherein, they say, you shine. Your sum of parts
Did not together pluck such envy from him
As did that one, and that, in my regard,
Of the unworthiest siege.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: What part is that, my lord?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
A very ribbon in the cap of youth,
Yet needful too, for youth no less becomes
The light and careless livery that it wears
Than settled age his sables and his weeds,
Importing health and graveness. Two months since,
Here was a gentleman of Normandy.
I’ve seen myself, and served against, the French,
And they can well on horseback. But this gallant
Had witchcraft in ’t. He grew unto his seat,
And to such wondrous doing brought his horse
As he had been encorpsed and demi-natured
With the brave beast. So far he topped my thought,
That I, in forgery of shapes and tricks,
Come short of what he did.

sentences.text: A Norman was ’t?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: A Norman.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: Upon my life, Lamond!

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: The very same.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
I know him well. He is the brooch indeed
And gem of all the nation.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
I know him well. He is the brooch indeed
And gem of all the nation.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
He made confession of you,
And gave you such a masterly report
For art and exercise in your defense,
And for your rapier most especially,
That he cried out ’twould be a sight indeed
If one could match you. The ’scrimers of their nation,
He swore, had had neither motion, guard, nor eye,
If you opposed them. Sir, this report of his
Did Hamlet so envenom with his envy
That he could nothing do but wish and beg
Your sudden coming o’er, to play with him.
Now, out of this—

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: What out of this, my lord?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
Laertes, was your father dear to you?
Or are you like the painting of a sorrow,
A face without a heart?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
Laertes, was your father dear to you?
Or are you like the painting of a sorrow,
A face without a heart?

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: Why ask you this?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
Not that I think you did not love your father
But that I know love is begun by time,
And that I see, in passages of proof,
Time qualifies the spark and fire of it.
There lives within the very flame of love
A kind of wick or snuff that will abate it.
And nothing is at a like goodness still.
For goodness, growing to a pleurisy,
Dies in his own too-much. That we would do,
We should do when we would, for this “would” changes
And hath abatements and delays as many
As there are tongues, are hands, are accidents.
And then this “should” is like a spendthrift sigh
That hurts by easing. —But to the quick of th’ ulcer:
Hamlet comes back. What would you undertake
To show yourself in deed your father’s son
More than in words?

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: To cut his throat i’ th’ church.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
No place, indeed, should murder sanctuarize.
Revenge should have no bounds. But, good Laertes,
Will you do this, keep close within your chamber.
Hamlet returned shall know you are come home.
We’ll put on those shall praise your excellence
And set a double varnish on the fame
The Frenchman gave you, bring you in fine together
And wager on your heads. He, being remiss,
Most generous and free from all contriving,
Will not peruse the foils; so that, with ease,
Or with a little shuffling, you may choose
A sword unbated, and in a pass of practice
Requite him for your father.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
I will do ’t.
And for that purpose I’ll anoint my sword.
I bought an unction of a mountebank,
So mortal that, but dip a knife in it,
Where it draws blood no cataplasm so rare,
Collected from all simples that have virtue
Under the moon, can save the thing from death
That is but scratched withal. I’ll touch my point
With this contagion, that if I gall him slightly
It may be death.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
Let’s further think of this,
Weigh what convenience both of time and means
May fit us to our shape. If this should fail,
And that our drift look through our bad performance,
‘Twere better not assayed. Therefore this project
Should have a back or second that might hold
If this should blast in proof.—Soft, let me see.—
We’ll make a solemn wager on your cunnings.—
I ha ’t! When in your motion you are hot and dry,
As make your bouts more violent to that end,
And that he calls for drink, I’ll have prepared him
A chalice for the nonce, whereon but sipping,
If he by chance escape your venomed stuck,
Our purpose may hold there. —But stay, what noise?

Let’s further think of this,
Weigh what convenience both of time and means
May fit us to our shape. If this should fail,
And that our drift look through our bad performance,
‘Twere better not assayed. Therefore this project
Should have a back or second that might hold
If this should blast in proof.—Soft, let me see.—
We’ll make a solemn wager on your cunnings.—
I ha ’t! When in your motion you are hot and dry,
As make your bouts more violent to that end,
And that he calls for drink, I’ll have prepared him
A chalice for the nonce, whereon but sipping,
If he by chance escape your venomed stuck,
Our purpose may hold there. —But stay, what noise?

GERTRUDE enters.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text:
One woe doth tread upon another’s heel,
So fast they follow.—Your sister’s drowned, Laertes.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text:
One woe doth tread upon another’s heel,
So fast they follow.—Your sister’s drowned, Laertes.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: Drowned? Oh, where?

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: Drowned? Oh, where?

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text:
There is a willow grows aslant a brook
That shows his hoar leaves in the glassy stream.
There with fantastic garlands did she come
Of crowflowers, nettles, daisies, and long purples,
That liberal shepherds give a grosser name,
But our cold maids do “dead men’s fingers” call them.
There, on the pendant boughs her coronet weeds
Clambering to hang, an envious sliver broke,
When down her weedy trophies and herself
Fell in the weeping brook. Her clothes spread wide,
And mermaid-like a while they bore her up,
Which time she chanted snatches of old lauds
As one incapable of her own distress,
Or like a creature native and indued
Unto that element. But long it could not be
Till that her garments, heavy with their drink,
Pulled the poor wretch from her melodious lay
To muddy death.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: Alas, then she is drowned.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: Drowned, drowned.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
Too much of water hast thou, poor Ophelia,
And therefore I forbid my tears. But yet
It is our trick. Nature her custom holds,
Let shame say what it will. When these are gone,
The woman will be out. —Adieu, my lord.
I have a speech of fire that fain would blaze,
But that this folly doubts it.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
Let’s follow, Gertrude.
How much I had to do to calm his rage!
Now fear I this will give it start again.
Therefore let’s follow.

Two GRAVEDIGGERS enter.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Is she to be buried in Christian burial when she
willfully seeks her own salvation?

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
I tell thee she is. Therefore make her grave straight.
The crowner hath sat on her and finds it Christian
burial.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
I tell thee she is. Therefore make her grave straight.
The crowner hath sat on her and finds it Christian
burial.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
I tell thee she is. Therefore make her grave straight.
The crowner hath sat on her and finds it Christian
burial.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
How can that be, unless she drowned herself in her own
defense?

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: Why, ’tis found so.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
It must be se offendendo. It cannot be else. For here
lies the point: if I drown myself wittingly, it argues
an act. And an act hath three branches—it is to act, to
do, to perform. Argal, she drowned herself wittingly.

do, to perform. Argal, she drowned herself wittingly.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: Nay, but hear you, Goodman Delver—

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Give me leave. Here lies the water. Good. Here stands
the man. Good. If the man go to this water and drown
himself, it is, will he nill he, he goes. Mark you that.
But if the water come to him and drown him, he drowns
not himself. Argal, he that is not guilty of his own
death shortens not his own life.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: But is this law?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: Ay, marry, is ’t. Crowner’s quest law.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: Ay, marry, is ’t. Crowner’s quest law.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Will you ha’ the truth on ’t? If this had not been a
gentlewoman, she should have been buried out o’
Christian burial.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Why, there thou sayst. And the more pity that great
folk should have countenance in this world to drown or
hang themselves more than their even Christian. Come, my
spade. There is no ancient gentleman but gardeners,
ditchers, and grave-makers. They hold up Adam’s
profession.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: Was he a gentleman?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: He was the first that ever bore arms.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: Why, he had none.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
What, art a heathen? How dost thou understand the
Scripture? The Scripture says Adam digged. Could he dig
without arms? I’ll put another question to thee. If thou
answerest me not to the purpose, confess thyself—

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: Go to.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
What is he that builds stronger than either the mason,
the shipwright, or the carpenter?

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
The gallows-maker, for that frame outlives a thousand
tenants.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
like thy wit well, in good faith. The gallows does
well, but how does it well? It does well to those that
do ill. Now thou dost ill to say the gallows is built
stronger than the church. Argal, the gallows may do well
to thee. To ’t again, come.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
“Who builds stronger than a mason, a shipwright, or a
carpenter?”

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: Ay, tell me that, and unyoke.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: Marry, now I can tell.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: To ’t.

sentences.speaker: SECOND GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: Mass, I cannot tell.
HAMLET and HORATIO enter, in the distance.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Cudgel thy brains no more about it, for your dull ass
will not mend his pace with beating. And when you are
asked this question next, say “A grave-maker.” The
houses that he makes last till doomsday. Go, get thee
in. Fetch me a stoup of liquor.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
[digs and sings]
In youth when I did love, did love,
Methought it was very sweet
To contract–o–the time, for–a–my behove,
Oh, methought, there–a–was nothing–a–meet.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Has this fellow no feeling of his business? He sings at
grave- making.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Has this fellow no feeling of his business? He sings at
grave- making.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: Custom hath made it in him a property of easiness.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
‘Tis e’en so. The hand of little employment hath the
daintier sense.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
‘Tis e’en so. The hand of little employment hath the
daintier sense.

[sings]
But age with his stealing steps
Hath clawed me in his clutch,
And hath shipped me into the land
As if I had never been such.
[throws up a skull]

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
That skull had a tongue in it and could sing once. How
the knave jowls it to the ground, as if it were Cain’s
jawbone, that did the first murder! It might be the pate
of a politician, which this ass now o’erreaches, one
that would circumvent God, might it not?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Or of a courtier, which could say, “Good morrow, sweet
lord!” “How dost thou, good lord?” This might be my Lord
Such-a-one that praised my Lord Such-a-one’s horse when
he meant to beg it, might it not?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Or of a courtier, which could say, “Good morrow, sweet
lord!” “How dost thou, good lord?” This might be my Lord
Such-a-one that praised my Lord Such-a-one’s horse when
he meant to beg it, might it not?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: Ay, my lord.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Why, e’en so. And now my Lady Worm’s, chapless and
knocked about the mazard with a sexton’s spade. Here’s
fine revolution, an we had the trick to see ’t. Did
these bones cost no more the breeding but to play at
loggets with them? Mine ache to think on ’t.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Why, e’en so. And now my Lady Worm’s, chapless and

[sings]
A pickax and a spade, a spade,
For and a shrouding sheet,
Oh, a pit of clay for to be made
For such a guest is meet.
[throws up another skull]

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
There’s another. Why may not that be the skull of a
lawyer? Where be his quiddities now, his quillities, his
cases, his tenures, and his tricks? Why does he suffer
this rude knave now to knock him about the sconce with a
dirty shovel and will not tell him of his action of
battery? Hum! This fellow might be in ’s time a great
buyer of land, with his statutes, his recognizances, his
fines, his double vouchers, his recoveries. Is this the
fine of his fines and the recovery of his recoveries,
to have his fine pate full of fine dirt? Will his
vouchers vouch him no more of his purchases, and double
ones too, than the length and breadth of a pair of
indentures? The very conveyances of his lands will
hardly lie in this box, and must the inheritor himself
have no more, ha?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: Not a jot more, my lord.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: Is not parchment made of sheepskins?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: Ay, my lord, and of calfskins too.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
They are sheep and calves which seek out assurance in
that.
I will speak to this fellow.—Whose grave’s this,
sirrah?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
They are sheep and calves which seek out assurance in
that.
I will speak to this fellow.—Whose grave’s this,
sirrah?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Mine, sir.
[sings]
Oh, a pit of clay for to be made
For such a guest is meet.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: I think it be thine, indeed, for thou liest in ’t.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
You lie out on ’t, sir, and therefore it is not yours.
For my part, I do not lie in ’t, and yet it is mine.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
You lie out on ’t, sir, and therefore it is not yours.
For my part, I do not lie in ’t, and yet it is mine.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Thou dost lie in ’t, to be in ’t and say it is thine.
‘Tis for the dead, not for the quick. Therefore thou
liest.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Thou dost lie in ’t, to be in ’t and say it is thine.
‘Tis for the dead, not for the quick. Therefore thou
liest.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Thou dost lie in ’t, to be in ’t and say it is thine.
‘Tis for the dead, not for the quick. Therefore thou
liest.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: ‘Tis a quick lie, sir. ‘Twill away gain from me to you.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: What man dost thou dig it for?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: For no man, sir.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: What woman, then?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: For none, neither.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: Who is to be buried in ’t?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
One that was a woman, sir, but, rest her soul, she’s
dead.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
How absolute the knave is! We must speak by the card,
or equivocation will undo us. By the Lord, Horatio,
these three years I have taken a note of it. The age is
grown so picked that the toe of the peasant comes so
near the heel of the courtier he galls his kibe. —How
long hast thou been a grave-maker?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Of all the days i’ the year, I came to ’t that day that
our last
King Hamlet overcame Fortinbras.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: How long is that since?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Cannot you tell that? Every fool can tell that. It was
the very day that young Hamlet was born, he that is mad
and sent into England.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Cannot you tell that? Every fool can tell that. It was
the very day that young Hamlet was born, he that is mad
and sent into England.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Cannot you tell that? Every fool can tell that. It was
the very day that young Hamlet was born, he that is mad
and sent into England.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: Ay, marry, why was he sent into England?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Why, because he was mad. He shall recover his wits
there, or, if he do not, it’s no great matter there.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Why, because he was mad. He shall recover his wits
there, or, if he do not, it’s no great matter there.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Why, because he was mad. He shall recover his wits
there, or, if he do not, it’s no great matter there.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Why, because he was mad. He shall recover his wits
there, or, if he do not, it’s no great matter there.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: Why?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
‘Twill not be seen in him there. There the men are as
mad as he.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
‘Twill not be seen in him there. There the men are as
mad as he.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: How came he mad?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: Very strangely, they say.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: How “strangely?”

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: Faith, e’en with losing his wits.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: Upon what ground?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Why, here in Denmark. I have been sexton here, man and
boy, thirty years.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Why, here in Denmark. I have been sexton here, man and
boy, thirty years.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: How long will a man lie i’ the earth ere he rot?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Faith, if he be not rotten before he die—as we have
many pocky corses nowadays that will scarce hold the
laying in— he will last you some eight year or nine
year. A tanner will last you nine year.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Faith, if he be not rotten before he die—as we have
many pocky corses nowadays that will scarce hold the
laying in— he will last you some eight year or nine
year. A tanner will last you nine year.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: Why he more than another?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Why, sir, his hide is so tanned with his trade that he
will keep out water a great while, and your water is a
sore decayer of your whoreson dead body. [indicates a
skull] Here’s a skull now. This skull has lain in the
earth three-and-twenty years.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
Why, sir, his hide is so tanned with his trade that he
will keep out water a great while, and your water is a
sore decayer of your whoreson dead body. [indicates a
skull] Here’s a skull now. This skull has lain in the
earth three-and-twenty years.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: Whose was it?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
A whoreson mad fellow’s it was. Whose do you think it
was?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
A whoreson mad fellow’s it was. Whose do you think it
was?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: Nay, I know not.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
A pestilence on him for a mad rogue! He poured a flagon
of Rhenish on my head once. This same skull, sir, was
Yorick’s skull, the king’s jester.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
A pestilence on him for a mad rogue! He poured a flagon
of Rhenish on my head once. This same skull, sir, was
Yorick’s skull, the king’s jester.

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text:
A pestilence on him for a mad rogue! He poured a flagon
of Rhenish on my head once. This same skull, sir, was
Yorick’s skull, the king’s jester.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: This?

sentences.speaker: FIRST GRAVEDIGGER
sentences.text: E’en that.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Let me see. [takes the skull] Alas, poor Yorick! I knew
him, Horatio, a fellow of infinite jest, of most
excellent fancy. He hath borne me on his back a thousand
times, and now, how abhorred in my imagination it is!
My gorge rises at it. Here hung those lips that I have
kissed I know not how oft. —Where be your gibes now?
Your gambols? Your songs? Your flashes of merriment that
were wont to set the table on a roar? Not one now to
mock your own grinning? Quite chapfallen? Now get you to
my lady’s chamber and tell her, let her paint an inch
thick, to this favor she must come. Make her laugh at
that. —Prithee, Horatio, tell me one thing.

excellent fancy. He hath borne me on his back a thousand
times, and now, how abhorred in my imagination it is!
My gorge rises at it. Here hung those lips that I have
kissed I know not how oft. —Where be your gibes now?
Your gambols? Your songs? Your flashes of merriment that
were wont to set the table on a roar? Not one now to
mock your own grinning? Quite chapfallen? Now get you to
my lady’s chamber and tell her, let her paint an inch
thick, to this favor she must come. Make her laugh at
that. —Prithee, Horatio, tell me one thing.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: What’s that, my lord?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Dost thou think Alexander looked o’ this fashion i’ th’
earth?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: E’en so.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: And smelt so? Pah! [puts down the skull]

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: And smelt so? Pah! [puts down the skull]

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: E’en so, my lord.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
To what base uses we may return, Horatio. Why may not
imagination trace the noble dust of Alexander till he
find it stopping a bunghole?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
To what base uses we may return, Horatio. Why may not
imagination trace the noble dust of Alexander till he
find it stopping a bunghole?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: ‘Twere to consider too curiously, to consider so.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
No, faith, not a jot. But to follow him thither with
modesty enough, and likelihood to lead it, as thus:
Alexander died, Alexander was buried, Alexander
returneth to dust, the dust is earth, of earth we make
loam —and why of that loam, whereto he was converted,
might they not stop a beer barrel?
Imperious Caesar, dead and turned to clay,
Might stop a hole to keep the wind away.
Oh, that that earth, which kept the world in awe,
Should patch a wall t’ expel the winter’s flaw!
But soft, but soft a while.

CLAUDIUS enters with GERTRUDE, LAERTES, and a coffin, with a PRIEST and other lords attendant.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Here comes the king,
The queen, the courtiers—who is this they follow,
And with such maimèd rites? This doth betoken
The corse they follow did with desperate hand
Fordo its own life. ‘Twas of some estate.
Couch we a while and mark.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: What ceremony else?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: That is Laertes, a very noble youth, mark.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: That is Laertes, a very noble youth, mark.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: What ceremony else?

sentences.speaker: PRIEST
sentences.text:
Her obsequies have been as far enlarged
As we have warranty. Her death was doubtful,
And, but that great command o’ersways the order,
She should in ground unsanctified have lodged
Till the last trumpet. For charitable prayers
Shards, flints and pebbles should be thrown on her.
Yet here she is allowed her virgin crants,
Her maiden strewments, and the bringing home
Of bell and burial.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: Must there no more be done?

sentences.speaker: PRIEST
sentences.text:
No more be done.
We should profane the service of the dead
To sing a requiem and such rest to her
As to peace-parted souls.

sentences.speaker: PRIEST
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
Lay her i’ th’ earth,
And from her fair and unpolluted flesh
May violets spring! I tell thee, churlish priest,
A ministering angel shall my sister be
When thou liest howling.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: [to HORATIO] What, the fair Ophelia?

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text:
Sweets to the sweet. Farewell! [scatters flowers]
I hoped thou shouldst have been my Hamlet’s wife.
I thought thy bride-bed to have decked, sweet maid,
And not have strewed thy grave.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
Oh, treble woe
Fall ten times treble on that cursèd head,
Whose wicked deed thy most ingenious sense
Deprived thee of! Hold off the earth awhile
Till I have caught her once more in mine arms.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
Now pile your dust upon the quick and dead,
Till of this flat a mountain you have made,
T’ o’ertop old Pelion or the skyish head
Of blue Olympus.

[comes forward] What is he whose grief
Bears such an emphasis, whose phrase of sorrow
Conjures the wandering stars, and makes them stand
Like wonder-wounded hearers? This is I,
Hamlet the Dane. [leaps into the grave]

[comes forward] What is he whose grief

Hamlet the Dane. [leaps into the grave]

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: The devil take thy soul!

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: The devil take thy soul!

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text: The devil take thy soul!

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Thou pray’st not well.
I prithee, take thy fingers from my throat,
For though I am not splenitive and rash,
Yet have I something in me dangerous,
Which let thy wisdom fear. Hold off thy hand.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: Pluck them asunder.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: Hamlet, Hamlet!

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: Hamlet, Hamlet!

sentences.speaker: ALL
sentences.text: Gentlemen—

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: [to HAMLET] Good my lord, be quiet.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: [to HAMLET] Good my lord, be quiet.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: [to HAMLET] Good my lord, be quiet.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Why, I will fight with him upon this theme
Until my eyelids will no longer wag.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: O my son, what theme?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
I loved Ophelia. Forty thousand brothers
Could not with all their quantity of love
Make up my sum. What wilt thou do for her?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
I loved Ophelia. Forty thousand brothers
Could not with all their quantity of love
Make up my sum. What wilt thou do for her?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
I loved Ophelia. Forty thousand brothers
Could not with all their quantity of love
Make up my sum. What wilt thou do for her?

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: O, he is mad, Laertes.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text: For love of God, forbear him.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
‘Swounds, show me what thou’lt do.
Woo’t weep? Woo’t fight? Woo’t fast? Woo’t tear
thyself?
Woo’t drink up eisel, eat a crocodile?
I’ll do ’t. Dost thou come here to whine,
To outface me with leaping in her grave?
Be buried quick with her?—and so will I.
And if thou prate of mountains let them throw
Millions of acres on us, till our ground,
Singeing his pate against the burning zone,
Make Ossa like a wart! Nay, an thou’lt mouth,
I’ll rant as well as thou.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text:
This is mere madness.
And thus a while the fit will work on him.
Anon, as patient as the female dove
When that her golden couplets are disclosed,
His silence will sit drooping.

And thus a while the fit will work on him.
Anon, as patient as the female dove
When that her golden couplets are disclosed,
His silence will sit drooping.

sentences.speaker: GERTRUDE
sentences.text:
This is mere madness.
And thus a while the fit will work on him.
Anon, as patient as the female dove
When that her golden couplets are disclosed,
His silence will sit drooping.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Hear you, sir.
What is the reason that you use me thus?
I loved you ever. But it is no matter.
Let Hercules himself do what he may,
The cat will mew and dog will have his day.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text: I pray thee, good Horatio, wait upon him.
HORATIO exits.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
[to LAERTES]
Strengthen your patience in our last night’s speech.
We’ll put the matter to the present push.—
Good Gertrude, set some watch over your son.—
This grave shall have a living monument.
An hour of quiet shortly shall we see.
Till then in patience our proceeding be.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
So much for this, sir. Now shall you see the other.
You do remember all the circumstances?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:

You do remember all the circumstances?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:

You do remember all the circumstances?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: Remember it, my lord?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Sir, in my heart there was a kind of fighting
That would not let me sleep. Methought I lay
Worse than the mutines in the bilboes. Rashly—
And praised be rashness for it: let us know
Our indiscretion sometimes serves us well
When our deep plots do pall, and that should teach us
There’s a divinity that shapes our ends,
Rough-hew them how we will—

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: That is most certain.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Up from my cabin,
My sea-gown scarfed about me, in the dark
Groped I to find out them, had my desire,
Fingered their packet, and in fine withdrew
To mine own room again, making so bold
(My fears forgetting manners) to unseal
Their grand commission, where I found, Horatio—
O royal knavery!—an exact command,
Larded with many several sorts of reasons
Importing Denmark’s health, and England’s too,
With—ho!—such bugs and goblins in my life
That, on the supervise (no leisure bated,
No, not to stay the grinding of the ax)
My head should be struck off.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: Is ’t possible?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
[shows HORATIO a document]
Here’s the commission. Read it at more leisure.
But wilt thou hear me how I did proceed?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: I beseech you.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Being thus benetted round with villainies—
Ere I could make a prologue to my brains,
They had begun the play—I sat me down,
Devised a new commission, wrote it fair.
I once did hold it, as our statists do,
A baseness to write fair, and labored much
How to forget that learning, but, sir, now
It did me yeoman’s service. Wilt thou know
Th’ effect of what I wrote?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: Ay, good my lord.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
An earnest conjuration from the king,
As England was his faithful tributary,
As love between them like the palm might flourish,
As peace should stiff her wheaten garland wear
And stand a comma ’tween their amities,
And many suchlike “as’s” of great charge,
That, on the view and knowing of these contents,
Without debatement further, more or less,
He should the bearers put to sudden death,
Not shriving time allowed.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: How was this sealed?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Why, even in that was heaven ordinant.
I had my father’s signet in my purse,
Which was the model of that Danish seal.
Folded the writ up in form of th’ other,
Subscribed it, gave ’t th’ impression, placed it
safely,
The changeling never known. Now, the next day
Was our sea fight, and what to this was sequent
Thou know’st already.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: So Guildenstern and Rosencrantz go to ’t.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Why, man, they did make love to this employment.
They are not near my conscience. Their defeat
Does by their own insinuation grow.
‘Tis dangerous when the baser nature comes
Between the pass and fell incensèd points
Of mighty opposites.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: Why, what a king is this!

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Does it not, think thee, stand me now upon—
He that hath killed my king and whored my mother,
Popped in between th’ election and my hopes,
Thrown out his angle for my proper life
(And with such cozenage!) —is ’t not perfect conscience
To quit him with this arm? And is ’t not to be damned
To let this canker of our nature come
In further evil?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:
It must be shortly known to him from England
What is the issue of the business there.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
It will be short. The interim’s mine.
And a man’s life’s no more than to say “one.”
But I am very sorry, good Horatio,
That to Laertes I forgot myself,
For by the image of my cause I see
The portraiture of his. I’ll court his favors.
But sure the bravery of his grief did put me
Into a towering passion.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: Peace.—Who comes here?
OSRIC, a young courtier, enters with his hat in his hand.

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text: Your lordship is right welcome back to Denmark.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
I humbly thank you, sir. [aside to HORATIO] Dost know
this water-fly?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: [aside to HAMLET] No, my good lord.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
[aside to HORATIO] Thy state is the more gracious, for
’tis a vice to know him. He hath much land, and fertile.
Let a beast be lord of beasts and his crib shall stand
at the king’s mess. ‘Tis a chough, but, as I say,
spacious in the possession of dirt.

spacious in the possession of dirt.

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text:
Sweet lord, if your lordship were at leisure, I should
impart a thing to you from His Majesty.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
I will receive it, sir, with all diligence of spirit.
Put your bonnet to his right use. ‘Tis for the head.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:

Put your bonnet to his right use. ‘Tis for the head.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text: I thank your lordship. It is very hot.

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text: I thank your lordship. It is very hot.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: No, believe me, ’tis very cold. The wind is northerly.

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text: It is indifferent cold, my lord, indeed.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
But yet methinks it is very sultry and hot for my
complexion.

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text:
Exceedingly, my lord. It is very sultry—as ’twere—I
cannot tell how. My lord, his majesty bade me signify to
you that he has laid a great wager on your head. Sir,
this is the matter—

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
I beseech you, remember— [indicates that OSRIC should
put on his hat]

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text:
Nay, good my lord, for mine ease, in good faith. Sir,
here is newly come to court Laertes, believe me, an
absolute gentleman, full of most excellent differences,
of very soft society and great showing. Indeed, to speak
feelingly of him, he is the card or calendar of gentry,
for you shall find in him the continent of what part a
gentleman would see.

absolute gentleman, full of most excellent differences,

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Sir, his definement suffers no perdition in you, though
I know to divide him inventorially would dizzy th’
arithmetic of memory, and yet but yaw neither, in
respect of his quick sail. But in the verity of
extolment, I take him to be a soul of great article, and
his infusion of such dearth and rareness as, to make
true diction of him, his semblable is his mirror. And
who else would trace him? His umbrage, nothing more.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET

The concernancy, sir? Why do we wrap the gentleman in
our more rawer breath?

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
The concernancy, sir? Why do we wrap the gentleman in

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text: Sir?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:
[aside to HAMLET] Is ’t not possible to understand in
another tongue? You will do ’t, sir, really.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO

[aside to HAMLET] Is ’t not possible to understand in

sentences.speaker: HAMLET

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text: Of Laertes?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:
[aside to HAMLET] His purse is empty already. All ’s
golden words are spent.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: Of him, sir.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text: I know you are not ignorant—

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
I would you did, sir. Yet in faith, if you did, it
would not much approve me. Well, sir?

I dare not confess that lest I should compare with him
in excellence, but to know a man well were to know
himself.

I mean, sir, for his weapon. But in the imputation laid
on him by them, in his meed he’s unfellowed.

sentences.speaker: OSRIC

sentences.text: What’s his weapon?

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text: Rapier and dagger.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: That’s two of his weapons. But well.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: That’s two of his weapons. But well.

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text:
The king, sir, hath wagered with him six Barbary
horses, against the which he has impawned, as I take it,
six French rapiers and poniards with their assigns—as
girdle, hangers, and so. Three of the carriages, in
faith, are very dear to fancy, very responsive to the
hilts, most delicate carriages, and of very liberal
conceit.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: What call you the carriages?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:
[aside to HAMLET] I knew you must be edified by the
margin ere you had done.

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text: The carriages, sir, are the hangers.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
The phrase would be more germane to the matter if we
could carry cannon by our sides. I would it might be
hangers till then. But, on: six Barbary horses against
six French swords, their assigns, and three
liberal-conceited carriages—that’s the French bet
against the Danish. Why is this “impawned,” as you call
it?

sentences.text:
The king, sir, hath laid that in a dozen passes between
yourself and him, he shall not exceed you three hits.
He hath laid on twelve for nine, and it would come to
immediate trial if your lordship would vouchsafe the
answer.

sentences.speaker: OSRIC

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: How if I answer “No”?

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text:
I mean, my lord, the opposition of your person in
trial.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Sir, I will walk here in the hall. If it please His
Majesty, ’tis the breathing time of day with me. Let the
foils be brought, the gentleman willing, and the king
hold his purpose. I will win for him an I can. If not, I
will gain nothing but my shame and the odd hits.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET

He does well to commend it himself. There are no
tongues else for ’s turn.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: This lapwing runs away with the shell on his head.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
He did comply, sir, with his dug before he sucked it.
Thus has he—and many more of the same bevy that I know
the drossy age dotes on—only got the tune of the time
and outward habit of encounter, a kind of yeasty
collection, which carries them through and through the
most fond and winnowed opinions; and do but blow them to
their trial, the bubbles are out.

A LORD enters.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
My lord, his majesty commended him to you by young
Osric, who brings back to him that you attend him in the
hall. He sends to know if your pleasure hold to play
with Laertes, or that you will take longer time.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
I am constant to my purpose. They follow the king’s
pleasure. If his fitness speaks, mine is ready, now or
whensoever, provided I be so able as now.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: In happy time.

sentences.speaker: LORD
sentences.text:
The queen desires you to use some gentle entertainment
to
Laertes before you fall to play.
The LORD exits.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: She well instructs me.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text: You will lose this wager, my lord.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
I do not think so. Since he went into France, I have
been in continual practice. I shall win at the odds. But
thou wouldst not think how ill all’s here about my
heart. But it is no matter.

sentences.text: Nay, good my lord—

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
It is but foolery, but it is such a kind of gain-giving
as would perhaps trouble a woman.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:
If your mind dislike anything, obey it. I will
forestall their repair hither and say you are not fit.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:

forestall their repair hither and say you are not fit.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Not a whit. We defy augury. There’s a special
providence in the fall of a sparrow. If it be now, ’tis
not to come. If it be not to come, it will be now. If it
be not now, yet it will come—the readiness is all.
Since no man of aught he leaves knows, what is ’t to
leave betimes? Let be.

CLAUDIUS enters with GERTRUDE, LAERTES, OSRIC, lords, and other attendants with trumpets, drums, fencing swords, a table, and pitchers of wine.

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
Come, Hamlet, come, and take this hand from me. [puts
LAERTES’ hand into HAMLET’s]

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Give me your pardon, sir. I’ve done you wrong.
But pardon ’t, as you are a gentleman.
This presence knows,
And you must needs have heard, how I am punished
With sore distraction. What I have done,
That might your nature, honor, and exception
Roughly awake, I here proclaim was madness.
Was ’t Hamlet wronged Laertes? Never Hamlet.
If Hamlet from himself be ta’en away,
And when he’s not himself does wrong Laertes,
Then Hamlet does it not. Hamlet denies it.
Who does it, then? His madness. If’t be so,
Hamlet is of the faction that is wronged.
His madness is poor Hamlet’s enemy.
Sir, in this audience,
Let my disclaiming from a purposed evil
Free me so far in your most generous thoughts
That I have shot mine arrow o’er the house
And hurt my brother.

His madness is poor Hamlet’s enemy.
Sir, in this audience,
Let my disclaiming from a purposed evil
Free me so far in your most generous thoughts
That I have shot mine arrow o’er the house
And hurt my brother.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Give me your pardon, sir. I’ve done you wrong.
But pardon ’t, as you are a gentleman.
This presence knows,
And you must needs have heard, how I am punished
With sore distraction. What I have done,
That might your nature, honor, and exception
Roughly awake, I here proclaim was madness.
Was ’t Hamlet wronged Laertes? Never Hamlet.
If Hamlet from himself be ta’en away,
And when he’s not himself does wrong Laertes,
Then Hamlet does it not. Hamlet denies it.
Who does it, then? His madness. If’t be so,
Hamlet is of the faction that is wronged.
His madness is poor Hamlet’s enemy.
Sir, in this audience,
Let my disclaiming from a purposed evil
Free me so far in your most generous thoughts
That I have shot mine arrow o’er the house
And hurt my brother.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
I am satisfied in nature,
Whose motive in this case should stir me most
To my revenge. But in my terms of honor
I stand aloof, and will no reconcilement
Till by some elder masters, of known honor,
I have a voice and precedent of peace
To keep my name ungored. But till that time
I do receive your offered love like love
And will not wrong it.

Your skill shall, like a star i’ th’ darkest night,

sentences.speaker: CLAUDIUS
sentences.text:
Set me the stoups of wine upon that table.
If Hamlet give the first or second hit
Or quit in answer of the third exchange,
Let all the battlements their ordnance fire!
The king shall drink to Hamlet’s better breath,
And in the cup an union shall he throw
Richer than that which four successive kings
In Denmark’s crown have worn. Give me the cups.
And let the kettle to the trumpet speak,
The trumpet to the cannoneer without,
The cannons to the heavens, the heavens to earth,
“Now the king dunks to Hamlet.” Come, begin.—
And you, the judges, bear a wary eye.

sentences.speaker: LAERTES
sentences.text:
It is here, Hamlet. Hamlet, thou art slain.
No medicine in the world can do thee good.
In thee there is not half an hour of life.
The treacherous instrument is in thy hand,
Unbated and envenomed. The foul practice
Hath turned itself on me. Lo, here I lie,
Never to rise again. Thy mother’s poisoned.
I can no more. The king, the king’s to blame.

sentences.text:
He is justly served.
It is a poison tempered by himself.
Exchange forgiveness with me, noble Hamlet.
Mine and my father’s death come not upon thee,
Nor thine on me. [dies]

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
Heaven make thee free of it. I follow thee.—
I am dead, Horatio. —Wretched queen, adieu!—
You that look pale and tremble at this chance,
That are but mutes or audience to this act,
Had I but time (as this fell sergeant, Death,
Is strict in his arrest), O, I could tell you—
But let it be. —Horatio, I am dead.
Thou livest. Report me and my cause aright
To the unsatisfied.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:
Never believe it.
I am more an antique Roman than a Dane.
Here’s yet some liquor left.
[lifts the poisoned cup]

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
As thou’rt a man,
Give me the cup. Let go! By heaven, I’ll have ’t.
[takes cup from HORATIO]
O God, Horatio, what a wounded name,
Things standing thus unknown, shall live behind me!
If thou didst ever hold me in thy heart
Absent thee from felicity a while,
And in this harsh world draw thy breath in pain
To tell my story.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text: What warlike noise is this?
OSRIC enters.

sentences.speaker: OSRIC
sentences.text:
Young Fortinbras, with conquest come from Poland,
To th’ ambassadors of England gives
This warlike volley.

sentences.speaker: HAMLET
sentences.text:
O, I die, Horatio.
The potent poison quite o’ercrows my spirit.
I cannot live to hear the news from England.
But I do prophesy the election lights
On Fortinbras. He has my dying voice.
So tell him, with th’ occurrents, more and less,
Which have solicited. The rest is silence.
O, O, O, O. [dies]

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:
Now cracks a noble heart.—Good night, sweet prince,
And flights of angels sing thee to thy rest!—
Why does the drum come hither?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO

Now cracks a noble heart.—Good night, sweet prince,
And flights of angels sing thee to thy rest!—
Why does the drum come hither?
FORTINBRAS and the English AMBASSADOR enter, with a drummer and attendants.

sentences.speaker: FORTINBRAS
sentences.text: Where is this sight?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:
What is it ye would see?
If aught of woe or wonder, cease your search.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:
What is it ye would see?
If aught of woe or wonder, cease your search.

sentences.speaker: FORTINBRAS
sentences.text:
This quarry cries on havoc. O proud death,
What feast is toward in thine eternal cell,
That thou so many princes at a shot
So bloodily hast struck?

sentences.speaker: FORTINBRAS
sentences.text:
This quarry cries on havoc. O proud death,
What feast is toward in thine eternal cell,
That thou so many princes at a shot
So bloodily hast struck?

sentences.speaker: AMBASSADOR
sentences.text:
The sight is dismal,
And our affairs from England come too late.
The ears are senseless that should give us hearing,
To tell him his commandment is fulfilled,
That Rosencrantz and Guildenstern are dead.
Where should we have our thanks?

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:
[indicates CLAUDIUS] Not from his mouth,
Had it th’ ability of life to thank you.
He never gave commandment for their death.
But since so jump upon this bloody question,
You from the Polack wars, and you from England,
Are here arrived, give order that these bodies
High on a stage be placèd to the view,
And let me speak to th’ yet-unknowing world
How these things came about. So shall you hear
Of carnal, bloody, and unnatural acts,
Of accidental judgments, casual slaughters,
Of deaths put on by cunning and forced cause,
And, in this upshot, purposes mistook
Fall’n on th’ inventors’ heads. All this can I
Truly deliver.

sentences.speaker: FORTINBRAS
sentences.text:
Let us haste to hear it,
And call the noblest to the audience.
For me, with sorrow I embrace my fortune.
I have some rights of memory in this kingdom,
Which now to claim my vantage doth invite me.

sentences.speaker: HORATIO
sentences.text:
Of that I shall have also cause to speak,
And from his mouth whose voice will draw on more.
But let this same be presently performed,
Even while men’s minds are wild, lest more mischance
On plots and errors happen.

sentences.speaker: FORTINBRAS
sentences.text:
Let four captains
Bear Hamlet like a soldier to the stage,
For he was likely, had he been put on,
To have proved most royally. And, for his passage,
The soldiers’ music and the rites of war
Speak loudly for him.
Take up the bodies. Such a sight as this
Becomes the field, but here shows much amiss.
Go, bid the soldiers shoot.
